item.text: Level 3 reuses the ring/selection visual language but changes the object structure: eight ring markers, one black selection cell, and two large same-colour movable structures. I’m testing whether the existing outline leg generalizes; if it fails, I’ll isolate the smallest reusable extension.

The three selectable objects are now identified experimentally: a horizontal segment, an X outline, and a diamond outline, all moving on a 3-pixel lattice. The eight ring centers lie on that same lattice. I’m solving the exact-cover placement that puts every ring center onto one of those outlines, using ring coverage as the dense progress measure.

item.text: The clone plan completed level 3 exactly. I’ve added a reusable leg that experimentally classifies the selected shapes, searches minimum-movement ring-cover placements, and executes them; `play_level_3` only composes that leg as required. I’m validating full replay now.
